title: Chloé Zhao's Golden Globes Acceptance Speech for Hamnet
text: The Focus Features film Hamnet won Best Film Drama at the 2026 Golden Globes, marking the production's second win of the evening following Jessie Buckley's best female actor award. In her acceptance remarks, director Zhao honored those who experienced losses during filming, acknowledging that the cast and crew remained mindful of them. She recalled guidance from her lead actor Paul Mescal, who emphasized that creating the film taught him about vulnerability and self-acceptance. The message was the most important thing about making the film was learning to be vulnerable enough to allow ourselves to be seen for who we are. Zhao highlighted the significance of the creative work being done by filmmakers in the room, specifically recognizing fellow nominee Ryan Coogler of Sinners. She concluded by praising her colleagues' courage and emotional generosity, stating: I see so many of you have become so strong and tender at the same time...So salute your bravery and your dedication. Producer Steven Spielberg had previously acknowledged Zhao during the award announcement, calling her the only filmmaker capable of bringing Maggie O'Farrell's novel to the screen.
